overview: June baselines make the human-relevance story clearer, but this still reads more like enabling biology than a mature intervention program.
next_stage_requirement: Replicated human biomarker or function evidence tied to genome-maintenance interventions.
key_question: Can a tractable human intervention improve aging-relevant outcomes without unacceptable tradeoffs?
review_reason: June 2026 editorial rollup after reviewed genomic-instability track baselines.

overview: Telomerase now has a human biomarker foothold, while telomere protection and capping remain animal-stage and safety-sensitive.
next_stage_requirement: Human biomarker or function data from clearly targeted telomere programs.
key_question: Can telomere-focused benefit be delivered without simply moving risk elsewhere?
review_reason: June 2026 editorial rollup after reviewed telomere restoration and protection baselines.

overview: Partial reprogramming still drives attention, while methylation and chromatin tracks keep the translational story mixed and early.
next_stage_requirement: Safe human programs that go beyond clocks and short-term signatures.
key_question: Will epigenetic restoration stay measurable when the endpoint becomes actual human function?
review_reason: June 2026 editorial rollup after reviewed epigenetic restoration and chromatin baselines.

overview: The hallmark now spans animal proteasome work, disease-specific chaperone signals, and protein-aggregate programs with real clinical endpoints.
next_stage_requirement: Human programs with interpretable aging-relevant functional endpoints.
key_question: Can proteostasis support become a generalized aging intervention rather than a disease-specific tool?
review_reason: June 2026 editorial rollup after reviewed proteostasis track baselines.

overview: Multiple autophagy-linked tracks now have human-adjacent signals, but pathway engagement still outruns durable outcome evidence.
next_stage_requirement: Repeated human evidence that autophagy engagement predicts durable benefit.
key_question: When does pathway engagement stop being interesting and start being enough?
review_reason: June 2026 editorial rollup after reviewed autophagy, lysosomal, and selective-autophagy baselines.

overview: This remains one of the most operationally mature hallmark areas, now with rapalog, AMPK, caloric-restriction-mimetic, and insulin/IGF baselines in view.
next_stage_requirement: Replicated human functional outcomes in older adults, not just pathway or biomarker movement.
key_question: Can the field translate mature biology into robust everyday functional benefit?
review_reason: June 2026 editorial rollup after reviewed nutrient-sensing track baselines.

overview: The hallmark has several human functional or biomarker footholds, but delivery and transplantation remain animal-led and durable outcomes are inconsistent.
next_stage_requirement: More durable human function evidence tied to specific mitochondrial programs.
key_question: Which mitochondrial signals actually predict healthy-lifespan gain?
review_reason: June 2026 editorial rollup after reviewed mitochondrial track baselines and coverage repair.

overview: Senolytics and senomorphics keep the clearest human-facing story, while immune clearance remains earlier and more platform-like.
next_stage_requirement: Independent human studies showing functional benefit with acceptable safety.
key_question: Does clearing or modulating senescent cells move meaningful human outcomes enough to matter?
review_reason: June 2026 editorial rollup after reviewed senomorphic and immune-clearance baselines.

overview: Replacement and transplantation now strengthen the functional case, but rejuvenation and niche-support evidence still does not generalize cleanly.
next_stage_requirement: Aging-relevant human endpoints that are not just narrow disease repair stories.
key_question: Can regenerative capacity be restored in a way that generalizes beyond one tissue or indication?
review_reason: June 2026 editorial rollup after reviewed stem-cell rejuvenation, replacement, and niche-support baselines.

overview: Circulating factors, extracellular vesicles, and neuroendocrine reset now form a clearer track map, but the systems scope still needs discipline.
next_stage_requirement: Sharper human programs with clearer signaling targets and outcomes.
key_question: Which systemic signals are causal enough to justify targeted intervention?
review_reason: June 2026 editorial rollup after reviewed intercellular-communication track baselines.

overview: Clinical outcome and immune-resilience signals keep this unusually legible, while resolution-pathway work remains more biomarker-level.
next_stage_requirement: Aging-specific interpretation of functional outcomes across populations and contexts.
key_question: Which anti-inflammatory gains are genuinely aging leverage rather than narrower disease management?
review_reason: June 2026 editorial rollup after reviewed inflammation and resolution-pathway baselines.

overview: The microbiome map is now broader: live biotherapeutics and metabolite restoration show narrow functional signals, while composition and ecosystem replacement remain bounded.
next_stage_requirement: Durable outcome links that go beyond microbiome composition snapshots.
key_question: Which microbiome interventions matter because they change aging, not just because they change the microbiome?
review_reason: June 2026 editorial rollup after reviewed dysbiosis baselines and microbiome-composition surveillance.
